topic: Al-Khwarizmi and the erasure of non-western origins in western knowledge
what: Jonny observed that 'algorithm' is simply Al-Khwarizmi's name, Latinized, and 'algebra' comes from the title of his book. These words are so embedded in western technical education that nobody asks where they came from. The Iranian/Islamic origins of foundational mathematics are systematically absent from western curricula, especially charged given current US-Iran geopolitical conflict.
why_interesting: The knowledge is being withheld even if not always deliberately — it becomes harder to dehumanize a place when you know the math underlying your computer came from there. Also: Al-Khwarizmi was himself a fan of Ptolemy, which means intellectual fascination with ancient knowledge is not a 'western' trait. The Islamic Golden Age was partly about preserving and extending Greek/Roman knowledge during centuries when Europe had lost access to it — so the 'western' canon is partly Iranian-curated.
